records.description: General Counsel at Meridian Corp. Primary client contact for the patent infringement litigation against Pinnacle Industries. Authorized to make litigation strategy decisions and approve settlement parameters on behalf of Meridian Corp.

VP Engineering at Meridian Corp. Key fact witness — led the engineering team that developed the patented precision manufacturing processes at issue. Expected to testify regarding the invention timeline, technical specifications, and the differences between Meridian's patented processes and Pinnacle's AutoForge implementation.

Lead Partner at Baker & Associates LLP heading the Meridian Corp patent infringement case. 18 years of IP litigation experience with a focus on manufacturing and technology patents. Named a 'Top 50 IP Litigator' by American Lawyer Magazine.

Senior Associate at Baker & Associates LLP. Responsible for claim construction analysis, prior art research, and deposition preparation in the Meridian v. Pinnacle patent litigation. Background in mechanical engineering before transitioning to law.

Senior Paralegal at Baker & Associates LLP supporting the Meridian v. Pinnacle litigation team. Manages document production, coordinates expert witness schedules, and maintains the litigation case file. 10 years of IP litigation support experience.

Lead Partner at Chen Law Group representing defendant Pinnacle Industries. Specializes in patent defense for technology and manufacturing companies in the Texas corridor. Known for successful invalidity defenses and claim construction arguments.

Senior Associate at Chen Law Group. Supporting David Kim on the Pinnacle Industries defense. Leads prior art investigation and coordinates technical discovery responses. Holds a master's degree in electrical engineering.

Independent expert witness and consulting engineer specializing in precision manufacturing processes. Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from MIT. 25 years of industry experience. Retained by plaintiff Meridian Corp to provide expert testimony on the technical aspects of the patented manufacturing processes and alleged infringement by Pinnacle's AutoForge system.

Independent expert witness and patent analyst with expertise in claims construction and infringement analysis for manufacturing technology patents. Ph.D. in Industrial Engineering from Stanford. Former USPTO patent examiner. Retained by defendant Pinnacle Industries to provide expert testimony on patent validity and non-infringement of the asserted claims.

United States District Judge, Northern District of California. Presiding over Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries, Case No. 3:2025-cv-04892. Appointed to the federal bench in 2018. Known for efficient case management in complex patent disputes and experience with technology-related intellectual property matters.

CEO of Pinnacle Industries. Founded the company in 2012 after a career in industrial automation. Oversaw the development and launch of the AutoForge product line that is the subject of Meridian Corp's patent infringement claims. May be called as a fact witness regarding business decisions and product development timelines.

In-House Counsel at Pinnacle Industries. Primary liaison between Pinnacle and outside counsel Chen Law Group. Manages Pinnacle's IP portfolio and coordinates internal document collection and privilege review for the Meridian litigation.
